Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn text PRIMARY KEY, title  CHAR(30) NOT NULL, author CHAR(30)  ) CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn  text, FOREIGN KEY (isbn)  REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE, serial_number INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(isbn,serial_number), weight INTEGER ,  bookcase INTEGER  )

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('2', 'abc',xyz'); INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('2', 'abc','def');

Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, isbn BIGINT(13)  PRIMARY KEY, );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn BIGINT(13), serial_number CHAR, weight INTEGER, bookcase INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY(isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY(isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE  );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn TEXT, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) ); CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn TEXT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE NOT DEFERRABLE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (  isbn TEXT,  title TEXT,  author TEXT ); CREATE TABLE Copy (  isbn TEXT,  serial_number TEXT,  weight INTEGER,  bookcase TEXT ); FOREIGN KEY (Copy.isbn) REFERENCES ();

Answer: INSERT INTO Book VALUES ('1234', 'Mysteries', 'Daniel'); INSERT INTO Book VALUES ('1234', 'Facts', 'Daniel');

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(isbn INT, title TEXT, author TEXT, unique(isbn), PRIMARY KEY(isbn));   CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn INT, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, FOREIGN KEY(isbn)REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE);

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn INT NOT NULL,  title TEXT NOT NULL,  author TEXT,  PRIMARY KEY(isbn),  UNIQUE(isbn) );   CREATE Table Copy( isbn INT NOT NULL, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE  CASCADE, PRIMARY KEY (serial_number),         UNIQUE (serial_number)     );

Answer: INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES(123, 'Hallo', 'Levi Peters');   INSERT INTO Book(isbn, title, author) VALUES(123, 'Doei', 'Levi Peters');

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT  );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn TEXT, serial number INT PRIMARY KEY, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn VARCHAR, title TEXT, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn VARCHAR, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book VALUES('1', 'title1', 'author1'); INSERT INTO Book VALUES('1', 'title2', 'author2');

Q Text: Create an assertion that makes sure that no bookcase in our library has more than 1,000,000 grams of books.

A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book (     isbn TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,     title TEXT NOT NULL,     author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy (     isbn TEXT,     FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE,      serial_number INT,     weight INT,     bookcase INT,     PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number) );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn text, title text, author text, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn text, serial_number INT, weight INT, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book(isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn TEXT, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn) );   CREATE TABLE Copy( isbn TEXT, serial_number INT, weight INT NOT NULL, bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE CASCADE );

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book( isbn VARCHAR(15) PRIMARY KEY UNIQUE, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn VARCHAR(15) NOT NULL, serial_number INTEGER NOT NULL, weight INTEGER NOT NULL, bookcase INTEGER, UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book SELECT '1234', 'Book_a', 'Dirk'; INSERT INTO Book SELECT '1234', 'Book_b', 'Klaas';

Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn VARCHAR(15) NOT NULL, serial_number INTEGER NOT NULL, weight INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(weight BETWEEN 10 AND 1000), bookcase INTEGER, UNIQUE (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book(Isbn INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, Title VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, Author VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);   CREATE TABLE Copy(isbn INT NOT NULL, Serial_Number INT NOT NULL, weight Decimal(10,2) NOT NULL, Bookcase INT, PRIMARY KEY(isbn, Serial_Number), FOREIGN KEY isbn REFERENCES book(isbn));

Q Text: A library is setting up a database with the following relational schema:     Book(isbn, title, author)    Copy(isbn, serial_number, weight, bookcase)  A book must have an isbn and a title. Several copies of each book can be present in the library, each with a unique serial number, a weight in grams. Each bookcase has a number. The following constraints apply to every change to the database:         1. Every book has a unique value for isbn.    2. For every copy C there exists a book that has C's isbn. A copy cannot be added to the database if a book with the copy's isbn does not exists in the table Book.     3. If the isbn of a book in the database changes, then the isbn of copiesof that book should also change (so those copies continue to belong to the same book).    4. The removal of a book must result in all its copies being deleted.      Give the SQL create statements for Book and Copy such that the above requirements are fulfilled. Use sensible data types or each attribute. Use key constraints and associated actions. Do not use triggers.   NB foreign keys need to be enabled explicitly in SQLite 3 using the command "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;". Without this option enabled, SQLite will ignore foreign keys, which will make it difficult to properly test your queries.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Book ( isbn TEXT PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT NOT NULL, author TEXT );   CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT, serial_number INTEGER, weight INTEGER, bookcase INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON UPDATE CASCADE ON DELETE CASCADE );

Q Text: Give a sequence of two insert statements that violate constraint 1 above ( Every book has a unique value for isbn).
Answer: INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('1', 'A', 'A'); INSERT INTO Book (isbn, title, author) VALUES ('1', 'A', 'A');

Q Text: Add a constraint to Copy (or recreate Copy with the added constraint) that makes sure that books never weigh less than 10 grams and never more than 1000 grams.
Answer: CREATE TABLE Copy ( isbn TEXT, serial_number INTEGER, weight INTEGER CHECK (weight >= 10 AND weight <= 1000), bookcase INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (isbn, serial_number), FOREIGN KEY (isbn) REFERENCES Book (isbn) ON DELETE CASCADE );
